collectiondata.overview:
Bhutan Tour Packages from Kolkata Starting from ₹28,000

At first light, mist drapes the Himalayan slopes in ethereal veils, and the silhouette of Taktsang—Bhutan’s famed Tiger’s Nest Monastery—emerges like a beacon of serenity. With Travelxploria as your trusted Best International Tour Operator, every step along the cliff-hanging path becomes a moment of awe. Through the Couple Package, an exclusive meditation session within the monastery’s ancient prayer halls invites inner calm, while the Best Honeymoon Package ensures intimate moments beneath fluttering prayer flags.

Descending into the emerald valley below, Thimphu awaits — its winding streets lined with wooden façades and smiling locals. Here, the colossal Buddha Dordenma towers above the capital. With Travelxploria’s Bhutan tour packages from Kolkata, you enjoy skip-the-line access and guided tours that blend culture, spirituality, and scenic beauty. Families can discover Bhutan’s traditions through hands-on mask-painting workshops, while couples capture memories beneath the soft glow of Himalayan sunsets.

The journey continues to Punakha Dzong, a masterpiece of Bhutanese architecture perched between the Mo Chhu and Po Chhu rivers. As you stroll across the wooden bridge, rose petals scatter in the wind — the perfect backdrop for honeymooners enjoying our Romantic Bhutan Package. For those seeking value without compromise, Travelxploria’s best-value Bhutan trip from Kolkata offers premium experiences at affordable prices, ensuring comfort, discovery, and authentic local flavor.

Northward lies the peaceful Phobjikha Valley, home to the black-necked cranes that glide across the dawn sky. In our Couple Package, you can enjoy a guided birdwatching trek and a stay in a cozy heritage farmhouse, where each window frames a living painting of Bhutan’s unspoiled countryside. For the culturally curious, Travelxploria’s Bhutan holiday packages coincide with the vibrant tsechu festivals, where monks perform masked dances to the rhythm of traditional drums — a once-in-a-lifetime sight for families and photographers alike.

Finally, your Bhutan journey closes in Paro with a rejuvenating hot stone bath scented with juniper and pine. Whether through our Honeymoon Package, Family Tour, or Adventure Itinerary, every Travelxploria trip is designed to balance luxury, spirituality, and authenticity — making us one of the best Bhutan tour operators from Kolkata.


Why Choose Travelxploria for Your Bhutan Tour

  • 7 Exclusive Bhutan Itineraries — Covering Paro, Thimphu, Punakha, Phuentsholing & Phobjikha Valley.

  • All-Inclusive Packages — Hotels, permits, transfers, meals & guided sightseeing.

  • Flexible Durations — 4-day, 5-day, 6-day, and 7-day Bhutan tours available.

  • Affordable Pricing — Starting from ₹28,000 per person.

  • Local Expertise — Bhutanese guides with years of experience ensure a seamless journey.


Our Top Bhutan Tour Packages from Kolkata

  1. 4 Days – Bhutan Short Escape
    Ideal for quick travelers — explore Phuentsholing and Paro with visits to monasteries and scenic viewpoints.

  2. 5 Days – Thimphu & Paro Classic
    Discover the heart of Bhutan with visits to Buddha Dordenma, Memorial Chorten, and Rinpung Dzong.

  3. 6 Days – Classic Bhutan Experience
    The most popular Bhutan package from Kolkata — includes Thimphu, Punakha, and Paro with local experiences.

  4. 7 Days – Himalayan Heritage Journey
    Ideal for families and culture lovers — explore Dochula Pass, Punakha Dzong, and folk museums.

  5. 6 Days – Luxury Bhutan Escape
    Stay in 4-star boutique resorts, enjoy fine dining, private transfers, and wellness experiences.

  6. 7 Days – Adventure in the Land of Thunder Dragon
    For thrill seekers — hiking trails, river valleys, and scenic drives across mountain passes.

  7. 5 Days – Romantic Bhutan Getaway
    Candlelight dinners, couple spa sessions, and serene monastery visits for an unforgettable honeymoon.



How to Reach Bhutan from Kolkata

By Air: Daily flights from Kolkata to Paro take approximately 1 hour 30 minutes via Druk Air or Bhutan Airlines.
By Road: Travelers can reach Phuentsholing via Siliguri (about 12 hours by car), a scenic drive through the foothills.


1. Classic Bhutan Tour Package from Kolkata

​

Perfect for first-time visitors, this package covers Paro, Thimphu, and Punakha—Bhutan’s cultural triangle. Walk through centuries of heritage at Paro Dzong, visit the Buddha Dordenma, and cross the Dochu La Pass for panoramic Himalayan views. All transfers, stays, and permits are included for a seamless Bhutan trip from Kolkata.


2. Bhutan Honeymoon Package from Kolkata

Designed for romance, this itinerary blends scenic stays and cozy experiences—candlelit dinners, couple spa therapies, and private visits to the Tiger’s Nest. Stay in boutique resorts surrounded by pine forests and enjoy personalized service throughout your journey.

3. Bhutan Family Tour Package from Kolkata

Ideal for families, this package includes interactive cultural workshops in Thimphu, gentle hikes around Paro, and kid-friendly stays with traditional Bhutanese meals. Enjoy safe transfers, experienced guides, and a pace suited for all ages.

4. Bhutan Adventure Tour Package from Kolkata

For thrill-seekers, this journey takes you off the beaten path—rafting on the Mo Chhu River, trekking across Phobjikha Valley, and cycling through Bhutan’s scenic mountain trails. Experience the wild beauty of Bhutan with full safety gear and local experts.

5. Bhutan Luxury Tour Package from Kolkata

Stay in 5-star resorts like Le Méridien or Amankora and enjoy private chauffeurs, curated dining, and helicopter sightseeing options. This is the ultimate way to experience Bhutan’s serenity without compromising on indulgence.

6. Bhutan Cultural & Festival Tour Package from Kolkata

Timed around Bhutan’s famous tsechu festivals, this package gives you front-row access to traditional mask dances, monastic rituals, and local artisan markets. It’s ideal for those who wish to witness Bhutan’s spiritual pulse up close.

7. Bhutan Budget Tour Package from Kolkata

Experience Bhutan affordably with our best-value holiday deal. Comfortable 3-star stays, key sightseeing, and guided tours of Paro and Thimphu—all starting from ₹28,000 per person. A perfect option for students, solo travelers, or weekend explorers.

We provide transparent pricing, personalized assistance, and 24x7 support.

In Thimphu, you can explore several significant landmarks, starting with the National Memorial Chorten, built in memory of the third Druk Gyalpo and dedicated to World Peace, a popular site for Buddhist religious festivals. The Buddha Dordenma Statue, located atop a hill, is a massive golden Buddha with 125,000 smaller Buddha statues inside, making it an iconic spot in the city. Changangkha Lhakhang, a 12th-century temple known as the \"wish-fulfilling\" temple, offers a stunning view of Thimphu. The Motithang Takin Preserve, home to Bhutan's national animal, the Takin, is a great destination for animal lovers. The National Library, established in 1967, houses a valuable collection of historic manuscripts, photos, and books. The Painting School, or Institute for Zorig Chusum, is where students learn traditional art forms, while the Bhutan Textile Museum showcases the country’s rich textile heritage. The Clock Tower, a unique architectural marvel with four clock faces, is a popular site, especially in the evening when visitors can enjoy the nearby restaurants and souvenir shops. Finally, Tashi Chho Dzong, a monastery on the banks of the Wang Chhu River, hosts the annual three-day Tsechu festival and dates back to 1216 AD. Overnight stay at the hotel in Thimphu.

Dochula Pass, located between Thimphu and Punakha at an elevation of around 3100 meters, offers a stunning view of the Eastern Himalayas and is home to 108 stupas. In winter, the pass is covered in snow, making it a particularly beautiful destination. Punakha Dzong, regarded as one of Bhutan's most beautiful architectural wonders, is situated at the confluence of the Pho Chu and Mo Chu rivers. It is a sacred site with deep historical significance and is especially suitable for first-timers, children, and elderly visitors. The Punakha Suspension Bridge, one of the oldest of its kind in the world, was built by the Buddhist monk Thangtong Gyalpo and is adorned with prayer flags. The bridge offers a picturesque spot for bird watching and nature photography. Overnight stay at the hotel in Paro.

Simtokha Dzong: Built in 1629 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal, Simtokha Dzong is one of the oldest dzongs in Bhutan. Also known asSangak Zabdhon Phodrang, it holds great historical and religious significance. Constructed in the 17th century, this ancient fortress is a remarkable example of Bhutanese architecture and remains an important cultural landmark.

National Museum of Bhutan: Once an ancient watchtower, this museum now houses an extensive collection of Bhutanese artifacts. From traditional costumes and armor to weapons and daily life tools, the museum reflects the rich heritage and artistic traditions of Bhutan.

Ta Dzong: Originally built in 1649 as a watchtower to defend the Rinpung Dzong during times of war, Ta Dzong was later converted into Bhutan’s National Museum in 1968. It now serves as a cultural treasure trove, offering insights into the country’s past.

Rinpung Dzong: Also known as Paro Dzong, this majestic fortress was constructed in 1644 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal. It is an excellent example of traditional Bhutanese architecture and is one of Paro’s top tourist attractions.

Drukgyal Dzong: Known as the “Victorious Fortress,” Drukgyal Dzong is a historic Buddhist monastery and fortress. It commemorates Bhutan’s victory over Tibetan invaders and is considered one of the most beautiful archaeological sites in the country.

Paro Airport Viewpoint: Paro International Airport, Bhutan’s only international airport, is famous for its scenic beauty. Surrounded by dramatic mountains and lush valleys, it is considered one of the most stunning airports in the world and a must-visit spot for travelers in Paro.

Kyichu Lhakhang: Also called Kyerchu Lhakhang, this sacred temple is one of the oldest and most revered pilgrimage sites in Bhutan. It is part of a network of 108 temples built by a Tibetan king and is a cherished spiritual destination known for its serene beauty.

  • Drive from Paro to Phuentsholing (6 hrs), evening free for local market. Overnight in Phuentsholing.
  • After breakfast, depart for NJP Station/Bagdogra Airport with cherished Bhutan holiday memories.

The National Memorial Chorten, built in memory of the third Druk Gyalpo, is dedicated to World Peace and is popular among locals for various Buddhist religious festivals. Another notable site in Thimphu is the Buddha Dordenma Statue, a massive golden Buddha atop a hill with 125,000 smaller Buddhas hidden inside, offering panoramic views and often referred to as Buddha Point. Changangkha Lhakhang, a 12th-century temple, is known as the wish-fulfilling temple and offers stunning views of Thimphu city. For animal lovers, the Motithang Takin Preserve is a great spot to see Bhutan's national animal, the takin, in a preserved setting. The National Library, established in 1967, houses historic manuscripts, photos, and books, while the Institute for Zorig Chusum, or Painting School, teaches traditional arts and crafts. The Bhutan Textile Museum showcases the rich history of Bhutanese textiles and clothing. The Clock Tower Square, with its four-faced clock tower, is an architectural marvel and a popular gathering spot with many nearby restaurants and souvenir shops. Tashi Chho Dzong, a monastery located next to the Wang Chhu River, is famous for its annual Tsechu festival and was built in 1216 A.D. Overnight stay at a hotel in Thimphu.

Dochula Pass, located between Thimphu and Punakha at an elevation of 3100 meters, offers stunning views of the Eastern Himalayas. The pass is home to 108 stupas, and in winter, it is covered in snow, creating a beautiful scene. The Punakha Dzong, situated at the confluence of the Pho Chu (male) and Mo Chu (female) rivers, is considered one of the most beautiful dzongs in Bhutan. It is particularly accessible for first-timers, children, and elderly visitors, and offers a chance to explore the sacred history of this architectural marvel. Another highlight in Punakha is the Punakha Suspension Bridge, one of the oldest suspension bridges in the world, believed to have been built by the Buddhist monk Thangtong Gyalpo. This historic bridge, adorned with prayer flags, provides a perfect spot for birdwatching and nature photography, making it renowned for its picturesque appearance. Overnight stay at a hotel in Paro.

Simtokha Dzong: Built in 1629 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal, Simtokha Dzong is one of the oldest dzongs in Bhutan. Also known asSangak Zabdhon Phodrang, it holds great historical and religious significance. Constructed in the 17th century, this ancient fortress is a remarkable example of Bhutanese architecture and remains an important cultural landmark.

National Museum of Bhutan: Once an ancient watchtower, this museum now houses an extensive collection of Bhutanese artifacts. From traditional costumes and armor to weapons and daily life tools, the museum reflects the rich heritage and artistic traditions of Bhutan.

Ta Dzong: Originally built in 1649 as a watchtower to defend the Rinpung Dzong during times of war, Ta Dzong was later converted into Bhutan’s National Museum in 1968. It now serves as a cultural treasure trove, offering insights into the country’s past.

Rinpung Dzong: Also known as Paro Dzong, this majestic fortress was constructed in 1644 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal. It is an excellent example of traditional Bhutanese architecture and is one of Paro’s top tourist attractions.

Drukgyal Dzong: Known as the “Victorious Fortress,” Drukgyal Dzong is a historic Buddhist monastery and fortress. It commemorates Bhutan’s victory over Tibetan invaders and is considered one of the most beautiful archaeological sites in the country.

Paro Airport Viewpoint: Paro International Airport, Bhutan’s only international airport, is famous for its scenic beauty. Surrounded by dramatic mountains and lush valleys, it is considered one of the most stunning airports in the world and a must-visit spot for travelers in Paro.

Kyichu Lhakhang: Also called Kyerchu Lhakhang, this sacred temple is one of the oldest and most revered pilgrimage sites in Bhutan. It is part of a network of 108 temples built by a Tibetan king and is a cherished spiritual destination known for its serene beauty.

packages.overview:
• Arrive at Hasimara, drive to Phuentsholing, complete permit formalities, and continue to Thimphu.
  • Full-day sightseeing in Thimphu, including Buddha Dordenma, National Memorial Chorten, Folk Heritage Museum, and Motithang Takin Preserve.
  • ​Drive to Phobjikha, visit Dochula Pass, Gangtey Monastery, Phobjikha Valley, and Black-Necked Crane Information Centre.
  • Drive to Bumthang via Trongsa, visit Pele La Pass, Chendebji Chorten, and Trongsa Dzong.
  • Full-day sightseeing in Bumthang, including Jakar Dzong, Kurje Lhakhang, Tamshing Lhakhang, Jambay Lhakhang, Mebar Tsho, Swiss Farm, and Red Panda Brewery.
  • Drive back to Punakha, passing through Trongsa and Pele La Pass.
  • Explore Punakha, including Punakha Dzong, Suspension Bridge, and Chimi Lhakhang, then drive to Paro.
  • Hike to Tiger's Nest Monastery, enjoy a hot stone bath, and relax in Paro.​
  • Explore Paro, visit the National Museum, and depart for Hasimara.​

Upon arrival at Hasimara Station, our company representative will greet you and drive you to Phuentsholing. After completing the permit formalities, you'll continue your journey to Thimphu. On the way, visit Kharbandi Gompa (Rinchending Monastery), located just outside Phuentsholing, which offers panoramic views of the town and the surrounding plains of India. It's a peaceful spot to begin your Bhutan journey. Afterward, check into your hotel, relax, and prepare for the upcoming journey.

After breakfast, embark on a full day of sightseeing in Thimphu, beginning with a visit to Buddha Dordenma, a massive statue overlooking the city and offering stunning panoramic views. Next, explore the National Memorial Chorten, a revered stupa built in honor of the third king where locals gather to pray and spin prayer wheels. Continue to the Folk Heritage Museum, which showcases traditional Bhutanese rural life through exhibits of farming tools, household objects, and a replica of a traditional farmhouse. In the afternoon, visit the Motithang Takin Preserve, home to Bhutan’s unique national animal, the takin. Then head to Changangkha Lhakhang, a popular temple where locals seek blessings for newborns, and conclude the day with a stroll around the iconic Clock Tower. Overnight stay will be in Thimphu.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Phobjikha","country":"Bhutan","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Thimphu to Phobjikha sightseeing & Stay Phobjikha.","day_summary":"

After breakfast, drive to the serene Phobjikha Valley with your first stop at Dochula Pass, located at an altitude of 3,100 meters and offering breathtaking views just 35 minutes from Thimphu. En route, visit the renowned Gangtey Monastery (Gangtey Goenpa), one of the oldest and most significant Nyingma monasteries in Bhutan, which overlooks the Phobjikha Valley and provides a tranquil atmosphere to explore traditional architecture and observe monks in prayer. Upon arrival in Phobjikha, enjoy the beauty of the glacial valley, known for its scenic landscapes, rich wildlife, and as a winter home for the endangered black-necked cranes migrating from Tibet between October and February. Conclude the day with a visit to the Black-Necked Crane Information Centre, which offers educational exhibits and spotting scopes to view the cranes during migration season, enhancing your understanding of the valley’s ecological importance. Overnight stay will be in Phobjikha.

Early in the morning, after breakfast, embark on a scenic drive to Bumthang via Trongsa, one of the most culturally rich and picturesque routes in Bhutan, typically taking 7 to 9 hours depending on stops and road conditions. Along the way, enjoy several key attractions including Pele La Pass at 3,420 meters, which separates western and central Bhutan and offers views of distant snow-capped peaks adorned with fluttering prayer flags. Stop at Chendebji Chorten, a serene Buddhist stupa modeled after Kathmandu’s Swayambhunath, perfect for a peaceful rest. Continue to Trongsa, where you’ll visit the majestic Trongsa Dzong, the largest fortress in Bhutan, historically significant and offering stunning views over the Mangde River. This route offers a captivating mix of culture, heritage, and natural beauty, culminating in an overnight stay at Bumthang.

After a healthy breakfast, begin a full day of sightseeing in Bumthang, the spiritual heartland of Bhutan, rich in history and culture. Start with a visit to Jakar Dzong, also known as the \"Castle of the White Bird,\" which offers panoramic views of the Bumthang Valley and serves as the administrative center of the region. Continue to Kurje Lhakhang, one of Bhutan’s most sacred monasteries where Guru Rinpoche meditated, followed by Tamshing Lhakhang, renowned for its ancient wall paintings and religious importance. Visit Jambay Lhakhang, a 7th-century temple built by Tibetan King Songtsen Gampo, making it one of the oldest and most significant temples in the country. Explore Mebar Tsho, or the Burning Lake, a revered holy site where Terton Pema Lingpa discovered hidden treasures. Conclude the day with a visit to the Swiss Farm to witness local cheese and cider production, and then enjoy a tasting at the Red Panda Brewery, Bhutan’s first brewery. Return to the hotel for an overnight stay.

Paro Taktsang, also known as the Tiger’s Nest Monastery, is one of the most sacred Vajrayana Buddhist sites in the Himalayas, dramatically perched on a cliffside in the upper Paro Valley of Bhutan. It is one of the thirteen legendary “Tiger’s Nest” caves across historical Tibet where Guru Padmasambhava is believed to have meditated and taught Vajrayana Buddhism.

The present-day monastery complex was constructed in 1692 around the Taktsang Senge Samdup cave, where Guru Padmasambhava, along with his disciples including Yeshe Tsogyal, engaged in deep spiritual practice before leaving Tibet in the early 9th century. Revered as the founder of Vajrayana Buddhism in Bhutan, Padmasambhava is also regarded as the country's spiritual protector.

Today, Paro Taktsang stands as the most iconic of these sacred caves, drawing pilgrims and travelers alike. After visiting this revered site, enjoy an overnight stay at your hotel in Paro.

packages.overview:
• Arrive at Bagdogra, complete immigration at Phuentsholing, and enjoy a scenic drive to Thimphu with enroute sightseeing including Milarepa Tower and Tashi Choedzong.
  • ​Excursion to Punakha with visits to Dochula Pass, 108 Chortens, Punakha Dzong, Suspension Bridge, and Chimi Lhakhang, then return to Thimphu.
  • ​Thimphu sightseeing including Buddha Viewpoint, Simply Bhutan, Wangdi Tse Hike, then transfer to Paro with a stop at Tachog Monastery.
  • Hike to the iconic Tiger’s Nest Monastery, visit a local beer factory, and enjoy the Paro Airport viewpoint.
  • ​Explore Kyichu Lhakhang and Rinpung Dzong, then drive to Phuentsholing for exit formalities and departure from Jaigaon.​

Simtokha Dzong: Built in 1629 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal, Simtokha Dzong is one of the oldest dzongs in Bhutan. Also known asSangak Zabdhon Phodrang, it holds great historical and religious significance. Constructed in the 17th century, this ancient fortress is a remarkable example of Bhutanese architecture and remains an important cultural landmark.

National Museum of Bhutan: Once an ancient watchtower, this museum now houses an extensive collection of Bhutanese artifacts. From traditional costumes and armor to weapons and daily life tools, the museum reflects the rich heritage and artistic traditions of Bhutan.

Ta Dzong: Originally built in 1649 as a watchtower to defend the Rinpung Dzong during times of war, Ta Dzong was later converted into Bhutan’s National Museum in 1968. It now serves as a cultural treasure trove, offering insights into the country’s past.

Rinpung Dzong: Also known as Paro Dzong, this majestic fortress was constructed in 1644 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal. It is an excellent example of traditional Bhutanese architecture and is one of Paro’s top tourist attractions.

Drukgyal Dzong: Known as the “Victorious Fortress,” Drukgyal Dzong is a historic Buddhist monastery and fortress. It commemorates Bhutan’s victory over Tibetan invaders and is considered one of the most beautiful archaeological sites in the country.

Paro Airport Viewpoint: Paro International Airport, Bhutan’s only international airport, is famous for its scenic beauty. Surrounded by dramatic mountains and lush valleys, it is considered one of the most stunning airports in the world and a must-visit spot for travelers in Paro.

Kyichu Lhakhang: Also called Kyerchu Lhakhang, this sacred temple is one of the oldest and most revered pilgrimage sites in Bhutan. It is part of a network of 108 temples built by a Tibetan king and is a cherished spiritual destination known for its serene beauty.

packages.overview:
• ​Arrival at Paro Airport, transfer to Thimphu with evening leisure and overnight stay.
  • ​Full-day sightseeing in Thimphu including Buddha Point, National Memorial Chorten, and Clock Tower Square.
  • Excursion to Phobjikha Valley and Gangtey Monastery, then overnight in Punakha.
  • ​Drive to Paro and visit the iconic Taktsang (Tiger’s Nest) Monastery.
  • Day trip to Chele La Pass and scenic Haa Valley, then return to Paro.
  • ​Full-day Paro sightseeing including Simtokha Dzong, National Museum, Rinpung Dzong, and Kyichu Lhakhang.
  • Departure from Paro with cherished Bhutan holiday memories.​

Begin your day in Thimphu with a visit to the National Memorial Chorten, built in memory of the third Druk Gyalpo and dedicated to world peace, widely frequented by locals during major Buddhist festivals. Continue to the iconic Buddha Dordenma Statue, a massive golden Buddha perched on a hilltop, housing 125,000 smaller statues and offering panoramic views of the city. Explore Changangkha Lhakhang, one of the oldest temples in Thimphu, dating back to the 12th century and revered as a wish-fulfilling temple with a stunning city view. Animal lovers will enjoy the Motithang Takin Preserve, home to the Takin—the national animal of Bhutan. Visit Tashichho Dzong, a striking monastery and fortress by the Wang Chhu River, known for hosting the annual three-day Tsechu festival and built in 1216 A.D. In the evening, take a stroll around Clock Tower Square, a popular local landmark featuring four clock faces, beautiful architecture, and surrounded by souvenir shops and restaurants. Return for an overnight stay at your hotel in Thimphu.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Punakha","country":"Bhutan","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Punakha to phobjika valley and Night stay in Punakha","day_summary":"

After breakfast, drive to Punakha and proceed for a full-day excursion to the scenic Phobjikha Valley, a vast glacial valley located at an elevation of approximately 3,000 meters (9,800 ft) on the western side of Bhutan’s Black Mountains, which rise above 5,000 meters (16,000 ft) and separate western and central Bhutan. The valley spans most of Phobji and Gangteng Gewogs and parts of Athang Gewog, and is renowned for its pristine natural beauty and cultural significance. It is home to the revered Gangteng Monastery, also known as Gangtey Gonpa, perched on a spur surrounded by the village of Gangtey. The valley also houses Ngenlung Drechagling, one of the eight sacred Lings established by the great Buddhist saint Longchenpa. After exploring the valley, return for an overnight stay at your hotel in Punakha.

to Paro And Night stay at paro Taktsang Monastery","day_summary":"

Paro Taktsang, also known as the Tiger’s Nest Monastery, is a sacred Vajrayana Himalayan Buddhist site dramatically perched on a cliffside in the upper Paro Valley of Bhutan. It is one of the thirteen Tiger’s Nest caves in historical Tibet where Guru Padmasambhava practiced and taught Vajrayana Buddhism. The current monastery complex was constructed in 1692 around the Taktsang Senge Samdup cave, where Padmasambhava meditated alongside his student Yeshe Tsogyal before leaving Tibet in the early 9th century. Revered as the spiritual founder of Bhutan, Padmasambhava is credited with introducing Vajrayana Buddhism to the region, and he remains the tutelary deity of the country. Today, Paro Taktsang stands as the most iconic of all the tiger lair caves, drawing pilgrims and visitors alike for its spiritual and architectural significance. Overnight stay will be at a hotel in Paro.

packages.overview:
• Arrive at Jaigaon, complete immigration, and enjoy a scenic drive through Gedu and Chukha to Thimphu with evening leisure and Tashi Choedzong view.
  • Full-day excursion to Punakha including Dochula Pass, Punakha Dzong, Chimi Lhakhang hike, and return to Thimphu.
  • Explore Thimphu highlights like Buddha Viewpoint and Simply Bhutan, then drive to Paro via Tachog Monastery.
  • Hike to the iconic Tiger’s Nest Monastery, visit the local beer factory and view Paro Airport.
  • Visit Kyichu Lhakhang and Rinpung Dzong, then drive to Jaigaon via Phuentsholing for departure assistance.

After enjoying breakfast at the hotel, begin your scenic drive to Punakha. En route, stop at the stunning Dochula Pass, located at an altitude of 3,150 meters, to take in panoramic Himalayan views and visit the iconic 108 Druk Wangyel Chortens. Continue onward to explore the majestic Punakha Dzong, a masterpiece of Bhutanese architecture nestled between two rivers. Take a walk across Bhutan’s longest suspension bridge, offering breathtaking valley views, followed by a short hike to Chimi Lhakhang, also known as the Temple of Fertility. Later, drive back to Thimphu where you can enjoy a leisurely evening exploring the town. Overnight stay will be in Thimphu.

The National Memorial Chorten in Thimphu was built in memory of the third Druk Gyalpo (King) of Bhutan and is dedicated to world peace. It is a revered site for locals and hosts several major Buddhist religious festivals, making it one of the most significant places to visit in Thimphu.

The Buddha Dordenma Statue, perched on a hilltop, is an iconic landmark in Thimphu. This grand golden statue of Buddha sits above a large meditation hall and houses 125,000 smaller Buddha statues inside, making it home to more Buddhas than the population of the city itself. It is commonly referred to as Buddha Point.

Another must-visit site is Changangkha Lhakhang, one of the oldest temples in Thimphu, dating back to the 12th century. Known as the \"wish-fulfilling temple,\" it holds deep religious significance. The temple also offers a stunning panoramic view of Thimphu city.

packages.overview:
• ​Arrival at Bagdogra Airport and transfer to Phuentsholing with scenic views of tea gardens.​
  • Immigration formalities and drive to Thimphu with en route photo stops and local sightseeing.​
  • Excursion to Punakha with visits to Dochula Pass, Suspension Bridge, Chimi Lhakhang & Punakha Dzong.​
  • Thimphu local sightseeing including Memorial Chorten, Buddha Dordenma, and Changangkha Lhakhang; drive to Paro.​
  • Hike to the iconic Tiger’s Nest Monastery (Paro Taktsang).
  • Paro sightseeing covering Simtokha Dzong, National Museum, Rinpung Dzong, Drukgyal Dzong, and Kyichu Lhakhang; return to Phuentsholing.
  • Departure from Phuentsholing to Bagdogra Airport with fond memories of Bhutan.​

The Buddha Dordenma Statue, perched on a hilltop, is an iconic landmark in Thimphu. This grand golden statue of Buddha sits above a large meditation hall and houses 125,000 smaller Buddha statues inside, making it home to more Buddhas than the population of the city itself. It is commonly referred to as Buddha Point.

Another must-visit site is Changangkha Lhakhang, one of the oldest temples in Thimphu, dating back to the 12th century. Known as the \"wish-fulfilling temple,\" it holds deep religious significance. The temple also offers a stunning panoramic view of Thimphu city.

Paro Taktsang, also known as the Tiger’s Nest Monastery, is one of the most sacred Vajrayana Buddhist sites in the Himalayas, dramatically perched on a cliffside in the upper Paro Valley of Bhutan. It is one of the thirteen legendary “Tiger’s Nest” caves across historical Tibet where Guru Padmasambhava is believed to have meditated and taught Vajrayana Buddhism.

The present-day monastery complex was constructed in 1692 around the Taktsang Senge Samdup cave, where Guru Padmasambhava, along with his disciples including Yeshe Tsogyal, engaged in deep spiritual practice before leaving Tibet in the early 9th century. Revered as the founder of Vajrayana Buddhism in Bhutan, Padmasambhava is also regarded as the country's spiritual protector.

Today, Paro Taktsang stands as the most iconic of these sacred caves, drawing pilgrims and travelers alike. After visiting this revered site, enjoy an overnight stay at your hotel in Paro.

Simtokha Dzong: Built in 1629 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal, Simtokha Dzong is one of the oldest dzongs in Bhutan. Also known as Sangak Zabdhon Phodrang, it holds great historical and religious significance. Constructed in the 17th century, this ancient fortress is a remarkable example of Bhutanese architecture and remains an important cultural landmark.

National Museum of Bhutan: Once an ancient watchtower, this museum now houses an extensive collection of Bhutanese artifacts. From traditional costumes and armor to weapons and daily life tools, the museum reflects the rich heritage and artistic traditions of Bhutan.

Ta Dzong: Originally built in 1649 as a watchtower to defend the Rinpung Dzong during times of war, Ta Dzong was later converted into Bhutan’s National Museum in 1968. It now serves as a cultural treasure trove, offering insights into the country’s past.

Rinpung Dzong: Also known as Paro Dzong, this majestic fortress was constructed in 1644 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal. It is an excellent example of traditional Bhutanese architecture and is one of Paro’s top tourist attractions.

Drukgyal Dzong: Known as the “Victorious Fortress,” Drukgyal Dzong is a historic Buddhist monastery and fortress. It commemorates Bhutan’s victory over Tibetan invaders and is considered one of the most beautiful archaeological sites in the country.

Paro Airport Viewpoint: Paro International Airport, Bhutan’s only international airport, is famous for its scenic beauty. Surrounded by dramatic mountains and lush valleys, it is considered one of the most stunning airports in the world and a must-visit spot for travelers in Paro.

Kyichu Lhakhang: Also called Kyerchu Lhakhang, this sacred temple is one of the oldest and most revered pilgrimage sites in Bhutan. It is part of a network of 108 temples built by a Tibetan king and is a cherished spiritual destination known for its serene beauty.

overview:
Bhutan Tour Packages from Kolkata Starting from ₹28,000

At first light, mist drapes the Himalayan slopes in ethereal veils, and the silhouette of Taktsang—Bhutan’s famed Tiger’s Nest Monastery—emerges like a beacon of serenity. With Travelxploria as your trusted Best International Tour Operator, every step along the cliff-hanging path becomes a moment of awe. Through the Couple Package, an exclusive meditation session within the monastery’s ancient prayer halls invites inner calm, while the Best Honeymoon Package ensures intimate moments beneath fluttering prayer flags.

Descending into the emerald valley below, Thimphu awaits — its winding streets lined with wooden façades and smiling locals. Here, the colossal Buddha Dordenma towers above the capital. With Travelxploria’s Bhutan tour packages from Kolkata, you enjoy skip-the-line access and guided tours that blend culture, spirituality, and scenic beauty. Families can discover Bhutan’s traditions through hands-on mask-painting workshops, while couples capture memories beneath the soft glow of Himalayan sunsets.

The journey continues to Punakha Dzong, a masterpiece of Bhutanese architecture perched between the Mo Chhu and Po Chhu rivers. As you stroll across the wooden bridge, rose petals scatter in the wind — the perfect backdrop for honeymooners enjoying our Romantic Bhutan Package. For those seeking value without compromise, Travelxploria’s best-value Bhutan trip from Kolkata offers premium experiences at affordable prices, ensuring comfort, discovery, and authentic local flavor.

Northward lies the peaceful Phobjikha Valley, home to the black-necked cranes that glide across the dawn sky. In our Couple Package, you can enjoy a guided birdwatching trek and a stay in a cozy heritage farmhouse, where each window frames a living painting of Bhutan’s unspoiled countryside. For the culturally curious, Travelxploria’s Bhutan holiday packages coincide with the vibrant tsechu festivals, where monks perform masked dances to the rhythm of traditional drums — a once-in-a-lifetime sight for families and photographers alike.

Finally, your Bhutan journey closes in Paro with a rejuvenating hot stone bath scented with juniper and pine. Whether through our Honeymoon Package, Family Tour, or Adventure Itinerary, every Travelxploria trip is designed to balance luxury, spirituality, and authenticity — making us one of the best Bhutan tour operators from Kolkata.


Why Choose Travelxploria for Your Bhutan Tour

  • 7 Exclusive Bhutan Itineraries — Covering Paro, Thimphu, Punakha, Phuentsholing & Phobjikha Valley.

  • All-Inclusive Packages — Hotels, permits, transfers, meals & guided sightseeing.

  • Flexible Durations — 4-day, 5-day, 6-day, and 7-day Bhutan tours available.

  • Affordable Pricing — Starting from ₹28,000 per person.

  • Local Expertise — Bhutanese guides with years of experience ensure a seamless journey.


Our Top Bhutan Tour Packages from Kolkata

  1. 4 Days – Bhutan Short Escape
    Ideal for quick travelers — explore Phuentsholing and Paro with visits to monasteries and scenic viewpoints.

  2. 5 Days – Thimphu & Paro Classic
    Discover the heart of Bhutan with visits to Buddha Dordenma, Memorial Chorten, and Rinpung Dzong.

  3. 6 Days – Classic Bhutan Experience
    The most popular Bhutan package from Kolkata — includes Thimphu, Punakha, and Paro with local experiences.

  4. 7 Days – Himalayan Heritage Journey
    Ideal for families and culture lovers — explore Dochula Pass, Punakha Dzong, and folk museums.

  5. 6 Days – Luxury Bhutan Escape
    Stay in 4-star boutique resorts, enjoy fine dining, private transfers, and wellness experiences.

  6. 7 Days – Adventure in the Land of Thunder Dragon
    For thrill seekers — hiking trails, river valleys, and scenic drives across mountain passes.

  7. 5 Days – Romantic Bhutan Getaway
    Candlelight dinners, couple spa sessions, and serene monastery visits for an unforgettable honeymoon.



How to Reach Bhutan from Kolkata

By Air: Daily flights from Kolkata to Paro take approximately 1 hour 30 minutes via Druk Air or Bhutan Airlines.
By Road: Travelers can reach Phuentsholing via Siliguri (about 12 hours by car), a scenic drive through the foothills.


1. Classic Bhutan Tour Package from Kolkata

​

Perfect for first-time visitors, this package covers Paro, Thimphu, and Punakha—Bhutan’s cultural triangle. Walk through centuries of heritage at Paro Dzong, visit the Buddha Dordenma, and cross the Dochu La Pass for panoramic Himalayan views. All transfers, stays, and permits are included for a seamless Bhutan trip from Kolkata.


2. Bhutan Honeymoon Package from Kolkata

Designed for romance, this itinerary blends scenic stays and cozy experiences—candlelit dinners, couple spa therapies, and private visits to the Tiger’s Nest. Stay in boutique resorts surrounded by pine forests and enjoy personalized service throughout your journey.

3. Bhutan Family Tour Package from Kolkata

Ideal for families, this package includes interactive cultural workshops in Thimphu, gentle hikes around Paro, and kid-friendly stays with traditional Bhutanese meals. Enjoy safe transfers, experienced guides, and a pace suited for all ages.

4. Bhutan Adventure Tour Package from Kolkata

For thrill-seekers, this journey takes you off the beaten path—rafting on the Mo Chhu River, trekking across Phobjikha Valley, and cycling through Bhutan’s scenic mountain trails. Experience the wild beauty of Bhutan with full safety gear and local experts.

5. Bhutan Luxury Tour Package from Kolkata

Stay in 5-star resorts like Le Méridien or Amankora and enjoy private chauffeurs, curated dining, and helicopter sightseeing options. This is the ultimate way to experience Bhutan’s serenity without compromising on indulgence.

6. Bhutan Cultural & Festival Tour Package from Kolkata

Timed around Bhutan’s famous tsechu festivals, this package gives you front-row access to traditional mask dances, monastic rituals, and local artisan markets. It’s ideal for those who wish to witness Bhutan’s spiritual pulse up close.

7. Bhutan Budget Tour Package from Kolkata

Experience Bhutan affordably with our best-value holiday deal. Comfortable 3-star stays, key sightseeing, and guided tours of Paro and Thimphu—all starting from ₹28,000 per person. A perfect option for students, solo travelers, or weekend explorers.

We provide transparent pricing, personalized assistance, and 24x7 support.
